summary: Power and wafer shortages will prevent an AI overbuild, enabling a smoother and longer AI investment cycle
summary_extended: Baker argues that the AI investment cycle will be structurally different from prior technology bubbles (railroads, internet) because physical supply constraints — specifically shortages of watts (power/energy) and wafers (TSMC capacity) — will prevent the overbuild that historically follows speculative manias. Drawing on Carlotta Perez's framework of technology cycles, he contends that these bottlenecks act as a natural governor on capital deployment, smoothing out the boom-bust dynamic that would otherwise follow. The investment implication is that AI infrastructure spending will be more sustained and less volatile than prior cycles, making it structurally bullish for AI infrastructure investors over a long time horizon. Notably, Baker does not name specific beneficiary companies — the thesis is a macro-structural view about cycle dynamics rather than a stock-specific call.
detail: Baker argues that historically, new technology bubbles lead to overbuilds and crashes (railroads, internet, etc.). The current constraints on watts (energy) and wafers (TSMC capacity) physically prevent the overbuild that would normally follow a bubble. This could result in a more sustained, less volatile AI investment cycle — structurally bullish for AI infrastructure investors.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as a core structural thesis with historical analogy (Carlotta Perez), stated with conviction as a reason to be constructive on AI infrastructure
quote_fragment: shortages of watts and wafers are going to keep us from having an overbuild

ticker_audit.reasoning: TSMC's wafer capacity is explicitly cited as one of the two key physical constraints (alongside power) that prevents an AI overbuild. TSM is a direct subject of the supply-side bottleneck thesis, though it is US-listed as an ADR.

NVDA is not mentioned in the thesis and is not a direct beneficiary of the supply-constraint argument in an unambiguous way — it could benefit from sustained demand but so could dozens of companies. The thesis is a macro cycle view, not a specific call on NVDA. Removing as tangential contamination.
